comment: Validated metrics from Grafana dashboards (cassandra_dashboard.json). Metrics collected from Cassandra exporter and chaos-toolkit instrumentation.

rate(db_cassandra_operation_count_total[.description: Cassandra operation execution rate per second (read + write operations combined)

rate(db_cassandra_read_latency_bucket[1m.description: Read operation latency histogram (use with histogram_quantile for percentiles)

rate(db_cassandra_write_latency_bucket[1.description: Write operation latency histogram (use with histogram_quantile for percentiles)

rate(db_cassandra_read_unavailables_tota.description: Rate of read unavailability errors (indicates cluster health and quorum issues)

rate(db_cassandra_write_unavailables_tot.description: Rate of write unavailability errors (indicates cluster health and quorum issues)

db_cassandra_native_connections.description: Number of native protocol connections to Cassandra cluster

extended_metrics.description: Extended metrics that may be available from Cassandra exporter (optional, for enhanced monitoring)

available_metrics.name: cassandra_compaction_completed_tasks_total
available_metrics.description: Total completed compaction tasks (use with rate())

available_metrics.name: cassandra_live_sstable_count
available_metrics.description: Number of live SSTables per table

available_metrics.name: cassandra_gossip_messages_sent_total
available_metrics.description: Total gossip messages sent (use with rate())

notes.read_write_latency_histogram: Both read and write latencies are histograms. Use histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(db_cassandra_read_latency_bucket[1m])) for 95th percentile read latency.
notes.cassandra_specific: Cassandra is a distributed database with read/write unavailables metrics. These indicate cluster health and quorum consensus issues.
notes.unavailables_explanation: Read/write unavailables occur when sufficient replicas aren't available to satisfy consistency level. >0.1/sec indicates potential cluster issues.
notes.connection_note: Cassandra native protocol connections scale with application load. Each connection can execute multiple requests.
notes.row_contention: Watch for latency increases during row-contention chaos tests. Cassandra row-level locking isn't as strict as relational databases.
notes.cluster_topology: These metrics assume a single-node or multi-node Cassandra cluster. Adjust baseline expectations based on cluster configuration.
notes.all_metrics_validated: All metrics in this file are validated against cassandra_dashboard.json Grafana dashboard
